free_text: Measurements of neutrons generated by deuterons
incident on thick Li targets
Department of Physics
Department of Fuels and Materials Research
Department of Energy Conversion Engineering,
Graduate School of Engineering Sciences
Experimental conditions and brief results
- Target is natural.
- Chemical-form of target is element.
- Physical-form of target is solid.
- Target-thickness: Natural Li metal in 10 mm (diam.)
x 30 mm cylindrical stainless case. Full stopping
length.
3 m (low En) and 8 m (high En) flight path
JAERI tandem accelerator
Data sets for lower and higher neutron data sets were
connected at 16 MeV < En < 20 MeV.
2 inch (diam.) x 2 inch NE213 (low En)
10 litre NE213 liquid scintillator (high En)
Total uncertainty considering
Detection efficiency (about 6%)
Dead time correction (about 2%)
Statistical uncertainty. For 3 m flight
path measurement, it was 2% at about 1
MeV, 1% at about 2 MeV, 0.2% at 10-20
MeV. For 8 m flight path measurement,
it was 0.5% at 20 MeV, 0.2% at 30 MeV,
0.5% at 40 MeV, 2-3% at maximum neutron
Neutron time-of-flight measurement system
Neutron detector and measured data analysis
Data received from authors
